evidence: Tested token_logprobs[0] across 7 distinct requests (5 non-streaming + 2 streaming) with varied prompts ('Hello world', 'The quick brown fox', 'What is 2+2?', 'Once upon a time in a far away land', 'Paris is the capital of', 'Hello world' streaming, 'The weather is' streaming)
All 7 requests returned token_logprobs[0] = null (JSON null)
Non-streaming requests used logprobs=1 with top_logprobs values of 3-5, max_tokens 5-20
Streaming requests correctly emitted token_logprobs[0]=null in the first chunk containing logprobs data
Evidence files: round2/req1_nonstreaming.json through req5_nonstreaming.json, round2/stream1_streaming.txt, round2/stream2_streaming.txt
Tested top_logprobs format across 74 total positions from 7 distinct requests (5 non-streaming + 2 streaming)
Position 0 (top_logprobs[0]) is always JSON null - confirmed across all 7 requests
Positions 1+ are always JSON objects (dicts) with string keys (token strings) and numeric values (logprob floats)
Request 1 (11 positions): pos 0=null, pos 1-10=dicts with str keys and numeric values
Request 2 (4 positions): pos 0=null, pos 1-3=dicts with str keys and numeric values
Request 3 (12 positions, top_logprobs=3): pos 0=null, pos 1-11=dicts with str keys and numeric values
Request 4 (21 positions, max_tokens=20): pos 0=null, pos 1-20=dicts with str keys and numeric values
Request 5 (6 positions): pos 0=null, pos 1-5=dicts with str keys and numeric values
Stream 1 (11 positions): pos 0=null, pos 1-10=dicts with str keys and numeric values
Stream 2 (9 positions): pos 0=null, pos 1-8=dicts with str keys and numeric values
Note: Some numeric values are JSON integers (e.g. 0 instead of 0.0) which is valid JSON - Python parses these as int type but they are semantically equivalent float values
Evidence files: round2/req1_nonstreaming.json through req5_nonstreaming.json, round2/stream1_streaming.txt, round2/stream2_streaming.txt

summary: Tested 2 assertions across 7 requests (5 non-streaming + 2 streaming) with 74 total top_logprobs positions: both PASS. VAL-LEG-002: token_logprobs[0] is null in all 7 requests. VAL-LEG-004: top_logprobs[0] is null in all 7 requests; positions 1+ are all dicts with string keys and numeric values.
